reason: Check frontend conventions, Vue 3 + Vite scope, and type import rule.
Check ranking request uses `/api/v1/rankings?period=week` with a request buffer of at least 10 items through API client semantics and does not use mock fallback data.
Check carousel CSS and markup preserve design-system tokens, focus states, motion rules, and warm community archive identity.
Check implementation satisfies requirements and acceptance criteria, especially weekly period and at least 10 requested items.
Check implementation follows planned data flow, feature-plus-rail UX, accessibility contracts, and scope boundaries.
Check all implementation checklist items, validation commands, review gates, and rollback considerations were completed.
Check the page requests weekly rankings with a buffer, filters to 10 displayable slides, maps real responses, and handles loading/empty/error states.
Check accessible carousel behavior, readable 10-item navigation, keyboard controls, and no cramped dot-only UX.
Check responsive layout, no horizontal overflow, token usage, and transform/opacity-only motion.
